I think there's more numbers to pull that help reiterate his first ballot resume.

19th on the All-Time Scoring List (25,728)

6th All-Time in 3-Pointers Made (2,290)

8-Time All-Star

10 consecutive seasons of averaging 20 or more points per game

2000 Olympic Gold Medalist

College All-American, 2x Final Four appearances

2000 Slam Dunk Champion

On the short list of players who've scored 50 points in a playoff game

NBA playoff record for 3-pointers in one half (8)

No numbers on this one but anecdotal - he's one of the most electric and exciting players of all-time, which I kind of feel plays a role in one's selection prospects from time to time (when in concert with a resume like Vince's).

Vince Carter owns the highest single-season point total in Raptors (1999-00) and Nets (2006-07) history. He is the only player to set the highest mark with multiple franchises since Wilt Chamberlain retired
